classObjective: Write linear equations in point-slope form given a point and a slope or two points and convert equations between point-slope, slope-intercept, and standard forms.
workedExamples: 5-2, Example 1 — Write Point-Slope Form Given a Point and Slope
5-2, Example 2 — Write Point-Slope Form Given Two Points
5-2, Example 3 — Convert Point-Slope Form to Slope-Intercept Form
5-2, Example 5 — Convert Equations to Standard Form
warmUp: Write point-slope form for the line through (3, -2) with slope 4
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: preserving integer coefficients in standard form; distributing slopes; form conversion strategies
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice writing equations in point-slope form and converting between all three linear forms
independentPractice: Practice problems writing equations in point-slope form and converting between point-slope, slope-intercept, and standard forms
assessment: Exit ticket: convert y + 3 = 2(x - 1) to slope-intercept and standard form
capReflection: What are the advantages of each linear form (point-slope, slope-intercept, standard) in different contexts?
